results.title: Secrets
results.creator_credit: Alan Lowery and Central Independent Television (UK)
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Last dream: a special report by John Pilger”.
Part two of John Pilger’s personal view of Australian history and identity. This part is about the past catching up with the present. Behind the Bicentennial ballyhoo, the original Australians are treated as badly as many blacks in South Africa; and, for an increasing number of whites, the old egalitarian ways no longer have a place in a media conscious “lucky country” which now produces fine wines, films and thousands of overnight millionaires whilst many of its children are living in poverty.

results.title: Other people's wars
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Last dream: a special report by John Pilger”.
The final episode of a three part series which explores aspects of Australia’s history. This part shows how Australians fight mostly against people with whom they have no quarrel and who offer them no threat of invasion. From the Boxer Rebellion in China to the Boer War in Africa, to the disaster in Vietnam, Australians have paid a unique blood sacrifice to appease a great protector.

results.title: Heroes Unsung
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Last dream: a special report by John Pilger”.
Part One of a three part series which explores aspects of Australia’s history. In this part, John Pilger traces the history of Australian immigration, from a personal perspective, from the horrors of the First Fleet through to the “White Australia” policy and the post-war “Populate or Perish” program. He challenges Australia’s treasured stereotypes and self-image and instead shows the great hardships suffered by marginalised and oppressed of Australia, in the process of building the nation.

results.title: Thomas and the jet engine
results.creator_credit: David Mitton, Britt Allcroft and Britt Allcroft Co
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Thomas the tank engine & friends”.
It’s full steam ahead for Thomas, the little blue engine and his special friends Gordon, Henry, James, Percy, Edward, Toby, Annie and Clarabel and their boss, the Fat Controller. The stories are narrated by Michael Angelis.

results.title: Thomas the Tank Engine; Thomas and Gordon (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: David Mitton, Britt Allcroft and Britt Allcroft Co
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Thomas the tank engine & friends”.
It’s full steam ahead for Thomas, the little blue engine with the short, stumpy funnel, short, stumpy boiler and short, stumpy dome and his special friends Gordon, Henry, James, Percy, Edward, Toby and Annie and Clarabel the carriages, and of course their boss, the Fat Controller. His friend, stroyteller Ringo Starr, says “Thomas is number one and never lets anyone forget it!” Based on The Railway Series by Rev W. Awdry.

results.title: Troilus and Cressida (BBC)
results.creator_credit: Jonathan Miller, BBC-TV and Time-Life Films
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “BBC Television Shakespeare Series”.
BBC adaptation of Shakespeare’s ‘Troilus and Cressida’, without doubt his most shockingly modern play. The Trojan war has been raging for seven years. Few care any more about its first cause, the abduction of Helen. Each side is consumed by its own in-fighting - in saving face and destroying others. What keeps them there is their love of honour and of violence. A tragically mismanaged sideshow to this squalid war, the love of Troilus and Cressida, becomes a pattern for all relationships destroyed by circumstance, misunderstanding and deceit. Cast includes Charles Gray, Anton Lesser, Tony Steedman and Suzanne Burden.

results.title: The Merry wives of Windsor
results.creator_credit: David Jones, Shaun Sutton and BBC
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “BBC Television Shakespeare Series”.
Adaptation of one of Shakespeare’s comedies. Falstaff in love, attempts to woo two wealthy burgesses of Windsor, whom in turn, resolve to trick him with all their female weaponry. A colourful, eccentric comedy featuring an excellent cast starring Richard Griffiths as Falstaff, Alan Bennett as Judge Shallow, Ben Kingsley as Frank Ford, and Judy Davis and Prunella Scales as Mistresses Ford and Page.

results.title: King Lear (Captioned)
results.creator_credit: Jonathan Miller, Shaun Sutton and BBC
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “BBC Television Shakespeare Series”.
This production of Shakespeare’s great play features Michael Hordern as King Lear, Michael Kitchen as Edmund, and Frank Middlemass as the Fool. “King Lear” is a tragedy that repeatedly portrays the restless intolerance of the young towards the old, the powerlessness of virtue in the face of deceit, and the fragility of the bonds which hold families and communities together. The production features an excellent Shakespearian cast.
